epic.arc: Every inference the loop runs becomes a routed, receipted, fallback-able act against an admitted mind. Today the loop has exactly one way to think — a bare `claude -p` inside the gate pen — and the day it was first exercised in anger it jammed twice (600s timeouts, issues #95/#96) and a session had to fill the seat by hand. The gateway is the local-first, AI-native answer: not LiteLLM (a dependency-heavy proxy with YAML for a brain) but what LiteLLM would be if it were a loop citizen — the mind registry (loop/minds.py, built and waiting) as the control plane of admitted, bdo-signed records, and one small gateway pen as the data plane: resolve mind -> backing reference -> one normalized completion call, every call a receipt (mind, prompt_hash, latency, tokens, outcome). Local is the privileged default (zero disclosure); an external family is the deliberate, receipted exception; a failed mind falls back by admitted rule, never by a stuck gate.
epic.context: Born from bdo's direction of 2026-06-12 ('we should probably build a local-first AI-native gateway... inspired by LiteLLM, not LiteLLM') the same day the single-provider seam double-timed-out. The substrate reserved every seam this needs before it was asked: loop/minds.py (atom.model-registry.v0, experience-layer wave 2) already admits minds with local/external families, secret-refusing backing references, and the odysseus:// scheme — bdo's own Odysseus workspace (Desktop/odysseus-sacrificial) serves local models via vLLM/llama.cpp/Ollama and its Cookbook does hardware-fit model selection, so Odysseus is a registered backing, never a fork; the trust ladder carries the local-model and external-mind classes with no rungs yet (the privileged default and the receipted exception, in code); the runs ledger and prompt_hash lineage make every past gate verdict replayable ground truth. One OpenAI-compatible client over stdlib covers Ollama, vLLM, llama.cpp, LM Studio, OpenRouter and OpenAI in one stroke; Anthropic/claude stays one named mind among many. Infrastructure-as-code is in scope at the right scale (bdo's clarification, 2026-06-12, reading the bare-claude-p failure: 'we need infra as code so we have better local support for multiple minds'): not Terraform for a fleet that does not exist, but declared, reproducible local serving — the admitted mind records are the desired state and a converge pen makes running reality match, the way .codex/ is rendered from fence/policy.py. D-2 grows the which-mind axis minds.py already names: a mind may not judge output its own backing wrote.
epic.value: The loop stops having a single point of thought. bdo chooses which minds may judge, from measurements instead of vibes; a jammed provider becomes a receipt and a fallback instead of a stuck seam and a hand-filled seat; local models carry the routine load at zero disclosure and zero marginal cost; and the same replay harness that picks the local model pays the inference-evals debt and is the convergence instrument epic.pivot needs.
epic.horizon: The loop runs its gates against minds it chose from its own measurements. Every inference in the repo goes through the gateway pen against an admitted mind; the local default carries the routine verdicts; claude and other external families judge only where the measurements say they must, as receipted exceptions; a mind that hangs or dies is a fallback receipt, not an incident; and bdo reads one surface — which mind judges which seam, at what agreement, latency and cost — and changes the routing with one stamp.

pieces.glue: the data plane: a small pen (outward reach lives in pens, never loop/) that resolves an admitted mind's backing reference (env: / http(s):// / odysseus:// / file:), makes one normalized OpenAI-compatible completion call over stdlib, and writes one receipt per call — mind id, prompt_hash, latency, tokens, outcome. Local-first in code: an external-family mind is refused unless the call names the receipted exception. No streaming until a surface needs it; a gate needs one completion.

the idle organ exercised: the first minds registered for real through loop.minds (bdo-signed — registering a backing is a disclosure event): the Odysseus/Ollama local backing the Cookbook recommends for this hardware, and the claude backing as a named external mind. The registry stops being dormant; the census reads it alive.

infra-as-code at laptop scale (bdo's clarification, 2026-06-12): the converge pen — the admitted mind records are the desired state, and one idempotent verb makes local reality match: ensure the named backing is alive (Ollama pull+serve, a docker-compose the pen renders from the records the way .codex/ is rendered from fence/policy.py, or Odysseus's serving API), health-check it, receipt every act, and report drift between declared and running. Level-triggered like the rest of the loop: the rendered artifacts are cache — delete them and the records rebuild them. A mind whose backing cannot be brought up reads as down on the drift report, never as a hung gate.

the gate pen's launch seam rerouted: gate.py composes the judging context exactly as today but inferences through the gateway against the mind the routing names, instead of a bare claude -p. The 600s double-timeout class of failure becomes a receipted, fallback-able event; D-2's which-mind axis enforced at the seam (a mind never judges its own backing's output).

the local-provider question answered by measurement: replay already-judged gate prompts (prompt_hash + verdicts on the log are ground truth) through each candidate local mind; report agreement, latency, and timeout-rate per mind per seam. Pays the inference-evals debt named in the five-gates arc; shares the convergence-across-independent-inferences shape epic.pivot's instrument needs. The output is a report bdo reads, and the routing he stamps cites it.

the LiteLLM-inspired part made AI-native: routing and fallback as admitted records — which mind for which seam, fallback order, budget caps — folded at call time by the gateway pen, superseded never erased. The control plane bdo steers with one stamp; no YAML, no config file outside the log.

the launch seam made real (bdo's home choice, 2026-06-20): the headless gate rail actually launches a verdict — explicit model (not the opus alias that 404s), a neutral cwd that dodges the repo's blocking hooks — and leaves a debuggable trace on every run. The precondition for measuring minds at all; landed #288.

the cost half of which-mind-from-measurements: the gate draws its model at random per run from a pool and the run ledger folds COST by model (cost-only first, impact deferred and never faked). A precursor on gate.py's launch to the replay-evals/routing-rules that run through the gateway pen.

the impact half: a configurable share of runs judge with ALL minds in parallel and compile unanimous-or-escalate, generating per-snapshot model comparisons — the cross-mind agreement measurement the horizon names ('at what agreement'), the room that tells whether a cheaper mind judges as well as the expensive one.

the judged data composed from config+policy (bdo's principle, 2026-06-20): the gate's facts (arc membership, confirmed status, self-claim reconciliation) are a deterministic fold, so only the judgment varies across minds — the clean, reproducible substrate the cross-mind cost-vs-impact measurements compare on.

the verdict-handling hardened on the launch/parse seam: a corrupted-.claude.json preamble on the headless child's stdout defeated the envelope unwrap and a real opus accept was lost double-escaped inside the envelope's result, escalating a clean 2-accept-1-amend by bug and burning $0.82. Two fixes: _verdict_objects recurses into an un-unwrapped envelope to recover the SAME structured verdict the mind emitted (never inferred from prose, the no-verdict/refuse path preserved), and a dry-run gate.py `recover` subcommand re-derives a panel from already-saved traces with ZERO new inference — so a parse bug is fixed AND the runs it broke are recovered without a paid re-run (bdo, 2026-06-24). Done-line 0194.
